page: Amazon Web Services g6f.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 vCPUs, consisting of 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core, running at a speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU providing 3 GB of VRAM, and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. The AMD EPYC processor achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25 percent, while multi-core performance is average. However, memory bandwidth is weak, scoring in the bottom 25 percent for both cached and uncached read and write operations. Similarly, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and text generation for LLM inference using both 135M and 7B models are poor, falling into the bottom 10 percent. Geekbench multi-core, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks also rank in the bottom 25 percent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g6f.xlarge offers entry-level access to hardware-accelerated graphics and local NVMe storage without the cost of a full GPU. The fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU provides a resource-dense option for lightweight graphics workloads or small-scale model inference. However, the tradeoff is constrained memory bandwidth and weak multi-threaded CPU scaling, which limits its suitability for heavy parallel processing or high-throughput database operations.

This instance is suited for lightweight GPU-accelerated workloads, basic graphics rendering, small-scale LLM prompt processing, and general-purpose tasks requiring local NVMe SSD storage. It is less suitable for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, or intensive multi-threaded compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g6f.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU providing 3 GB of VRAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, but weak memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling. The fractional GPU configuration offers a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight accelerated workloads, though it presents tradeoffs in parallel processing and memory throughput. This instance is best suited for lightweight graphics rendering, small-scale LLM prompt processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring modest GPU acceleration and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6f.xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU with 3 GB VRAM, and 100 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for lightweight accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6f.xlarge features 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 100 GB NVMe SSD storage for lightweight graphics workloads.
tagline: An entry-level accelerated instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, a fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU, and local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB DDR4 memory
Fractional NVIDIA L4 GPU providing 3 GB of VRAM
100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong single-core CPU performance but constrained memory bandwidth
Suitable for lightweight graphics rendering and small-scale LLM prompt processing
